ops.title: ccx web outline/read/search shipped 2026-07-08 — architecture, live-verified facts, and what remains unverified

ops.body:
`ccx web` (outline/read/search) shipped 2026-07-08: in-process engine in internal/web (OpEdit-style dispatch special-case, NOT a router backend), fetch cascade jina→exa→firecrawl→plainHTTP→browserbase, heading-tree chunking (768-token len/4 ceiling), hybrid BM25 + potion-base-8M embeddings via `uv run --with model2vec` subprocess (warm ≈0.54 s), gzipped-JSON page cache with 24h TTL, `§2.3#hash` cites (anchor.Of Crockford base32, NOT base36), guards in plugin/hooks/web_guards.py.

Live-verified: **keyless jina returns 401 in production** (the "keyless 20 RPM tier" is dead — cascade falls to plain HTTP, logged at Debug); PerimeterX/DataDome sensor scripts appear on normally-served 200 pages, so raw-HTML challenge detection needs the TIGHT marker set (challenge-page strings only), loose markers ("_px", "datadome") are safe only on cleaned markdown tiers.

Live-verified with real keys (2026-07-08, v0.8.1; keys persisted in gitignored cc-context/.env; committed key-gated tests in internal/web/live_test.go, off unless CCX_WEB_LIVE / CCX_WEB_LIVE_BROWSERBASE): all four hosted tiers reached and fixed where wrong. **jina**: `data.warning` is an INFORMATIONAL channel (cache-snapshot notices, soft "maybe CAPTCHA" hints), NOT an error channel — the tier hard-failed on any status-less warning and discarded valid 200 content; fixed to serve when content present, warning decisive only when content empty. Real jina failures come via HTTP status (a 404 target → jina 422, cascades). **browserbase** `/v1/fetch`: markdown is in `content` (NOT `markdown`), target status in `statusCode`; tier read the wrong field → empty for every stealth fetch; fixed. **BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID is NOT required** (API key alone → 200). **exa** `includeHtmlTags` DOES yield tagged HTML (go.dev → 61 sections) — correct as-is. **firecrawl** v2 `data.markdown`/`data.metadata.title` correct as-is. Note: bot-protected sites (ticketmaster/zillow) served fine via jina; the challenge→browserbase path stays hard to exercise live.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[ccx-exec-monty-notarization]] (the embed driver mirrors that subprocess pattern).
